title: Spinning on a top-whorl drop spindle
subtitle: The park-and-draft method for first-time spinners
excerpt: A top-whorl drop spindle is a weighted shaft that stores twist while you pull fibre into yarn. This tutorial teaches park-and-draft first, which separates the spinning and drafting actions so both hands can learn their roles before being asked to coordinate.
sourceNotes: Drop spindle construction and the park-and-draft learning method are consistent across handspinning instruction. Observation-led.

glossaryTerms.definition: The disc near the top of a drop spindle that acts as a flywheel, storing rotational momentum to keep the spindle turning between spin inputs.
A short length of pre-spun yarn tied to the spindle shaft to begin spinning. New fibre is joined to the leader, which carries the first twist into the new yarn.
To pull fibres apart lengthways to thin them before they receive twist. Controlling the draft zone determines the thickness of the finished yarn.
A beginner spindle method in which the spindle is held stationary under the arm while both hands draft fibre. Stored twist runs into the drafted zone when the spindle is released.

content.text: A top-whorl spindle has a weighted
whorl
near the hook end. Spin the shaft, let it drop on its
leader
, and the whorl stores enough energy to insert twist into the fibre you
draft
above. The difficulty is co-ordinating both actions simultaneously. The
park-and-draft
method separates them so each hand learns its role before they work together.
What you need

items.name: Leader yarn (60 cm of commercially spun singles or plied yarn)
Merino or BFL roving, pre-drafted into a thin rope

content.text: Method
Step 1: Attach the leader

Tie the leader yarn to the spindle shaft just below the whorl with a half-hitch. Wind 20 cm of leader up the shaft in the spinning direction (clockwise for Z-twist), bring it over the whorl, and loop it twice around the hook. Leave 30 cm of leader hanging free below the hook. This free length is where new fibre will join.
Step 2: Join roving to the leader

Thin the end of the roving by pulling its fibres apart, then overlap the thinned end with the last 5 cm of leader. Hold both together between your palms and roll briefly to integrate the fibres. The join should feel continuous rather than lumpy. Tug it: it should resist moderate tension without pulling apart.
Step 3: Park and draft

Spin the spindle clockwise with your dominant hand, then clamp the shaft under your non-dominant arm to park it. With both hands free, pinch the leader-roving join firmly with your bottom hand and pull roving upward with your top hand to draft a 10-15 cm zone of thin fibre. Release the spindle from under your arm: stored twist runs up through the pinch into the drafted zone and forms yarn. Wind the new yarn onto the shaft and repeat.
Step 4: Move to continuous spinning

Once park-and-draft feels reliable, try without parking. Spin the spindle and let it drop freely. Pinch just below the roving supply to stop twist entering the undrafted zone. Draft a short length downward toward the spindle. Release the pinch to let twist enter, re-pinch just above the new yarn, and draft again. The rhythm is: spin, pinch, draft, release, repeat.
Step 5: Wind on and continue

When spun yarn reaches the hook, unhook it, wind the new yarn onto the cop (the growing bundle on the shaft) in a roughly conical shape, and re-hook. A conical cop keeps the spindle balanced. A top-heavy cop causes wobble, which makes consistent spinning harder.

attrs.heading: Common problems
attrs.intro: Three problems beginners encounter with drop spindle spinning.

items.symptom: Yarn breaks and the spindle falls
items.cause: Too little twist was in the yarn before drafting pulled it apart.
items.fix: Let the spindle spin for longer before drafting. The yarn should feel firm and resist further drafting before you move the pinch upward.

items.symptom: Lumpy in some places, thin in others
items.cause: Inconsistent drafting: some sections were pulled further than others before receiving twist.
items.fix: Focus on drafting to the same length each time. Count mentally (draft for two counts, release for one) until the motion becomes consistent.

items.symptom: Spindle reverses direction and untwists the yarn
items.cause: The spindle flipped to counter-clockwise spin.
items.fix: Stop, re-spin clockwise, and continue. Always spin in the same direction throughout a single, or the twist will cancel itself.
